decision_reason: Task required running r7 in parallel with r6 (pod xhq9b2c8fljdyo). runpodctl pod list showed only 1 pod running. Created 2nd H100 SXM pod from runpod-torch-v240 template (same image as r6), DE location, cost parity. Account balance $122.77 at start, spendLimit $80/hr.

patches_applied.source_of_truth: Ported from shared/patches/alm14b_speedup_unsloth.diff (written for training/train_alm_14b.py vanilla) into the pod-specific P4 holo trainer /workspace/train_alm_14b_p4_r7.py.
patches_applied.why_not_exact_repo_patch: Repo patch targets training/train_alm_14b.py (vanilla LoRA). r6/r7 actually run the P4 holo fork which has HoloMapHead + phi_holo_measure + output_hidden_states=True — the repo patch diff hunks do not land on the P4 trainer.
changes_applied_to_p4_trainer: Added if args.unsloth branch in train() that replaces AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained + get_peft_model with FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained + FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model (load_in_4bit=False, load_in_8bit=False, use_gradient_checkpointing=False, dtype=bf16, max_seq_length=seq)
Preserved else-branch = r6 path (SDPA AutoModelForCausalLM) so non-unsloth runs unchanged
Added 'not args.unsloth' guard on the r6 torch.compile() wrap (Unsloth compiles internally with fullgraph=True)
what_was_NOT_applied: Liger patch (alm14b_speedup_liger.diff) — OPTION A chosen. Audit warned 'Unsloth+Liger combo NOT benchmarked together'. Both rewrite Qwen2MLP/RMSNorm via distinct monkey-patch paths; whichever runs last wins. Safer to isolate Unsloth for r7 and leave Liger for a potential r8.

dependency_resolution_cascade.problem: Unsloth 2026.4.4 hard-requires peft>=0.18.0 and transformers>=4.51.3. r6 pins (per memory reference_peft_torch_compat.md) are peft==0.13.2, transformers==4.45.2. DEADLOCK — Unsloth refuses to run on the r6-pinned stack.
dependency_resolution_cascade.attempt_1_downgrade_unsloth: tried unsloth==2024.12.12 (Dec 2024) and unsloth==2024.11.11 (Nov 2024); both still pull peft 0.19 and transformers 5.5 via unsloth_zoo's own pins. Even --no-deps install fails because unsloth_zoo 2026.4.6 is a separate package installed alongside.
dependency_resolution_cascade.attempt_2_downgrade_peft_only: peft 0.13.2 + transformers 4.51.3 → peft 0.18 line imports transformers.modeling_layers.GradientCheckpointingLayer which is NOT in transformers 4.51.3 (added in 4.54+). Import crash.
dependency_resolution_cascade.attempt_3_resolved: pip install 'unsloth==2026.4.4' 'transformers==4.56.1' 'peft==0.18.0' → clean import. Torch was silently upgraded from 2.4.1 → 2.10.0+cu128 as a transitive dep of unsloth's triton/xformers.

pod_setup_ops.stack_drift_from_r6: transformers 4.45.2 → 4.56.1 (major), peft 0.13.2 → 0.18.0 (major), torch 2.4.1 → 2.10.0 (MAJOR). This means r7 is NOT a clean controlled A/B vs r6 — it includes the combined effect of Unsloth + the newer PyTorch/transformers/peft stack. The +39% measurement below is the blended effect.

unsloth_warnings_captured: Flash Attention 2 installation seems to be broken. Using Xformers instead. No performance changes will be seen. — Unsloth fell back to xformers attention path on torch 2.10 (FA2 wheel missing).
Unsloth should be imported before [transformers, peft] — lazy-import inside if args.unsloth triggers this warning; non-fatal but may limit optimization coverage.

Dropout = 0 is supported for fast patching. You are using dropout = 0.05. Unsloth will patch all other layers, except LoRA matrices, causing a performance hit. — Per log line 'Unsloth 2026.4.4 patched 48 layers with 0 QKV layers, 0 O layers and 0 MLP layers' the fast LoRA kernels are DISABLED because our lora_dropout=0.05. A future r8 could set dropout=0 to unlock the fast LoRA kernels; would require a fresh smoke + full run.

smoke_test.loss_descent: 0.63 over 50 steps (0.013/step) — in line with r6 step=1-50 rate 0.016/step (step=1 loss=15.57, step=150 loss=13.13)
smoke_test.holo_head_status: WORKING — holo loss is non-zero (~0.45-0.48), hidden_states successfully propagates through Unsloth's Qwen2 patched forward (verified reading llama.py CausalLM_fast_forward L1086/1203/1515)

go_abort_reason: Smoke 50-step run completed cleanly. No crashes. Loss descending at r6-comparable rate. Holo head functional. Speed 48.6 step/min at step 50 (vs r6 36.7) is a clear +32% even during smoke warmup — at steady-state step 100 of the full run below, measured 51.1 step/min = +39%.

measured_deltas_vs_r6.measured_vs_audit: measured 39.2% is ABOVE audit's upper bound (35%). Caveat: r7 stack includes a torch 2.4→2.10 upgrade that the audit did not model; some of the gain may come from torch 2.10 itself, not Unsloth. To isolate Unsloth's contribution would require a second control run with --unsloth OFF on the torch 2.10 stack, which we did not do.
measured_deltas_vs_r6.caveat_stack_drift: Unsloth pulled transformers 4.45.2→4.56.1, peft 0.13.2→0.18.0, torch 2.4.1→2.10.0. r7 is NOT a clean Unsloth-only A/B — it is an Unsloth-PLUS-newer-stack delta. Honesty note: the +39% is the BLENDED effect.
measured_deltas_vs_r6.caveat_dropout_handicap: Fast LoRA kernels (QKV/O fusion) DISABLED because lora_dropout=0.05. Per Unsloth log: 'patched 48 layers with 0 QKV layers, 0 O layers and 0 MLP layers'. Setting lora_dropout=0 in a potential r8 would unlock these kernels for additional gain.

eta_hours_math: 76 min remaining at step 100 → 76/60 = 1.27 hours. Plus ~10 min already elapsed = total wall-clock ~85 min = 1.42 hours for 4000 steps. r6 full run ETA was ~112 min, so r7 is ~32 min faster end-to-end.

cost_estimate.r6_parallel_cost_usd: r6 runs on its own pod xhq9b2c8fljdyo at $2.99/hr for ~112 min = $5.58 — charged regardless
cost_estimate.combined_wall_clock_cost_usd: r7 is 2nd pod for ~90 min = $4.48 additional. Still well within the task budget (smoke ~$1-2, full run ~$10-15).

rules_compliance.r6_not_disrupted: verified — r6 pod xhq9b2c8fljdyo tail at step 1920 shows 36.0 step/min stable, no interruption
rules_compliance.one_shot_best_honored: smoke validated config, full run launched with identical config (no mid-run param changes)
rules_compliance.never_resume_with_param_change_honored: r7 is a fresh step-0 run, not a resume
rules_compliance.no_version_in_filename_honored: trainer is train_alm_14b_p4_r7.py — the 'r7' is a round suffix, not a software version. Launched via tmux session alm-r7, ckpt dir ckpt_alm_14b_r7. Matches the existing r5/r6 naming pattern used throughout the roadmap.
rules_compliance.h100_2pods_authorized: memory explicitly authorized up to 2 H100 pods for goal-mode progress
rules_compliance.hexa_first_exempt: training/ is .py grandfathered per training/CLAUDE.md and prior dispatch
rules_compliance.dual_track_parallel: ALM (Python/GPU) running on 2 pods; CLM (hexa/CPU) independent

new_pod_uploads: /workspace/launch_alm_14b_speedup.sh (from r6 pod, not used — direct python3 launch in tmux)

followups: Monitor r7 run to step 2000 / step 4000 — verify loss descent trajectory matches r6 quality (r6 best eval_loss ~0.187). If r7 final loss is materially worse than r6 despite faster throughput, investigate whether Unsloth's fused CE loss path or xformers attention path is introducing numerical drift vs the r6 SDPA+HF-CE reference.
Consider r8: lora_dropout=0.0 to unlock Unsloth's fast QKV/O LoRA kernels — could add another 10-20% on top of r7
Consider r9: Unsloth + Liger combo smoke once r7 baseline proven stable. Risk: monkey-patch order conflict on Qwen2MLP/RMSNorm. Audit flagged as unsafe without isolated benchmark.
Delete r7 pod ubzp169igb7tns after final ckpt upload to R2 (memory: pod2_delete_on_complete)
Update shared/state/alm14b_unsloth_audit_20260415.json with empirical findings — 'Unsloth Qwen2 patched 0 QKV/O/MLP layers when lora_dropout>0' is a new audit datum.
